CPO cable thesis / public semiconductor relationship map

100 public semiconductor names, 2,000+ mapped value-chain connections, and an alpha-score leaderboard.

This HTML report researches the claim that the CPO cable layer is the cheapest and most underappreciated part of the co-packaged-optics value chain. It maps 50 US-headquartered and 50 non-US public semiconductor ecosystem companies, their direct value-chain relationships, major disclosed cash-flow events, and a heuristic alpha ranking.

Input thesis: "In the CPO value chain, the category that is currently the cheapest, has the greatest upside, and remains underappreciated by the market is CPO cables."
Verdict: structurally plausible for the function, but not uniformly cheap in public equities after the 2025-2026 optics rerating.

Executive conclusion

The cable/interconnect layer is real: NVIDIA and Broadcom both describe CPO ecosystems that require optical engines, lasers, fiber attach, sockets, pluggable laser sources, OSAT processes, and system-level integration. The cable idea is attractive because every new high-radix AI network pushes more bandwidth through the physical interconnect stack.

The catch: the cleanest public semiconductor beneficiaries are no longer all cheap. Lumentum, Coherent, Credo and Astera have already repriced aggressively. The better alpha hunt is now in second-order suppliers: 200G/lane optical analog, silicon-photonics substrates, metrology/probe, packaging equipment, specialty foundry and small photonics platforms.

Top alpha bucket in this model: MACOM, Semtech, Lumentum, Coherent, Credo, Astera, Soitec, Aixtron, Nova, Camtek, BESI, Technoprobe, Veeco, Onto, FormFactor and Photronics. These are not recommendations; they are research targets where the valuation-vs-inflection question is most interesting.

Important limitation

Most semiconductor supplier/customer relationships do not disclose pairwise dollar revenue. This report flags each relationship as:

Specific public named relationship or transaction; Public value-chain public product/customer category but no pairwise dollars; Inferred adjacency structural supply-chain link to investigate before trading.

The graph is a research map, not a verified purchase-order ledger. Use SEC filings, annual reports, earnings transcripts and customer concentration disclosures before committing capital.

Alpha leaderboard

Alpha score is a 0-100 heuristic: CPO/AI-interconnect exposure, under-discovery, revenue-inflection evidence, scarcity/pure-play value, and risk-adjusted valuation. The score is not a price target and does not predict future returns.

CPO value-chain mind map

The cable thesis is not only about literal fiber. Public semiconductor alpha can appear in every physical layer required to replace copper reach with optics.

1. Compute / switch ASIC

GPU, XPU, switch ASIC, SerDes, die-to-die and HBM interfaces.

NVDAAVGOMRVL

2. Electrical-to-optical

DSPs, drivers, TIAs, retimers, AEC/AOC, LPO/NPO/CPO bridge chips.

CRDOALABMTSI

3. Photonics + light

Lasers, external light sources, PICs, optical engines, SiPho modules.

LITECOHRPOET

4. Packaging + test

Hybrid bonding, OSAT, probe, burn-in, metrology, known-good optical/electrical die.

AMKRBESIFORM

5. Materials + equipment

SOI/substrates, wafers, photomasks, etch/deposition, inspection.

SOI.PAENTGNVMI

Role heat map

Average alpha by role. Highest scores tend to cluster in optical components, signal integrity, small equipment, metrology, probe and substrates.

Interactive relationship graph

Every company has at least 20 direct mapped connections. Use the depth control to expand up to five hops. Direct edges in the table show who is using, buying from, depending on, or economically exposed to whom.

Source trail

Primary sources were prioritized where available. Market-cap and revenue snapshots are approximate and should be refreshed before making any investment decision.
